free_text: Neutron resonance spectroscopy for n+204Pb total and
differential elastic scattering cross sections
ORELA
Electrons on tantalum target.
NE110 scintillators.
Corrected for dead time and for constant room
background.
No reply from R.F.Carlton to author proof.
Two cylindrical samples with a thickness of 0.1378
atom/b, enriched to 71.41% 204Pb, alternated into and
and out of the beam with a sample of 88.33% 206Pb and
and a sample of natural Pb. Results corrected for
overcompensation for 208Pb, resulting in an inverse
sample thickness of 10.155 b/atom for Pb.  204Pb
represents 99.44% of sample, resulting in a thickness
of 10.2118 b/atom for 204Pb.
201.575-m flight path.
Absolute uncertainty
Data produced from J. Harvey files by D. Wiarda.
Cylindrical sample with thickness of 0.1378 atom/b,
enriched to 71.41% 204Pb, suspended in center of
evacuated scattering chamber.
200.191-m flight path.
Not corrected for multiple scattering.
Unable to obtain data; data at 90 and 160
degrees given in Fig. 1 of reference.
Analysis of transmission data using R-matrix
code SAMMY. Scattering data used to deduce spins and
parities of associated levels.
Resonances at 24.1, 27.5, and 32.8 keV were included
in analysis; energies and widths taken from
compilation, Mughabghab, Neutron Resonance Parameters
and Thermal Cross Sections, Part B, Vol. 1 (Academic
Press, Orlando, 1984).
Resonance energy uncertainties vary from 0.02% at 50
keV to 0.05% at 1 MeV.
Uncertainties in widths vary from about 5% for the
larger resonances to 10% for the smaller ones with a
minimum uncertainty of 0.5 eV.
Data taken from Table I in reference.
Full list of resonance data not received.
